data.description:
## Finding (S10 — verified)
`replay_events()` has control-flow nesting depth 19 (deepest in repo), in the event-replay correctness core; the file grew this window (659->761).

Citations: `src/rebar/reducer/_processors.py:648` (`replay_events`, depth 19).

## Remediation
Mechanical extract-method: move each event-type branch body VERBATIM into its own `_apply_<EVENT>(state, event)` handler; replace the nested if/elif tower with a dispatch that calls the handler by event type; use guard/early-continue clauses. Behavior-identical (handler bodies are the same code; dispatch selects the same branch). Guarded by the reducer's replay tests (same input events -> same final state).

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `replay_events` is a shallow dispatch loop (depth <= ~4) over named per-event handlers
- [ ] Reducer replay tests pass unchanged (byte-identical final state before/after)
- [ ] Each handler holds the same logic as its former branch
data.title: Flatten replay_events (depth 19) into per-event handler dispatch
